# loopkit

> A self-governed coding loop for any git repository: declare a goal and two shell-command gates,
> and it drives a coding agent toward the goal tick by tick — committing every tick to an isolated
> branch, verified by an in-sample iteration gate, a held-out acceptance gate, and an on-by-default
> adversarial review judge, bounded by hard stops (budget / no-progress / iteration cap) and a
> safety envelope (never `main`, protected paths, opt-in-only push). Python; core deps are only
> `typer + rich + pydantic`. The same loop scales to a parallel batch, a Redis-queue fleet, and a
> Kubernetes control plane.

Start with the AI primer — it is the one-page structured map (module → contract table, invariants,
full command + config surface, extension seams) written for machine readers:

## Docs

- [AI primer](docs/AI-PRIMER.md): the dense canonical map — read this first
- [README](README.md): what it is, install, the 5-step Quickstart, the two gates
- [Using on your repo](docs/USING-ON-YOUR-REPO.md): end-to-end operator guide (remote sync, issues, CI, batch)
- [Control files](docs/CONTROL-FILES.md): every `loopkit.toml` / `PROMPT.md` knob, annotated
- [Config reference example](examples/gates/loopkit.example.toml): fully-commented starter config
- [Architecture wiki](docs/architecture/README.md): how it's built; module-ownership map

## Operations

- [Operating a run](docs/OPERATING.md): silent-vs-hung, resume, output per tier
- [Troubleshooting](docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md): common failures and what they mean
- [Billing](docs/BILLING.md): which credential pays; subscription vs API key

## Optional

- [Default judge design](docs/default-judge-design.md): the built-in review judge
- [Docs index](docs/README.md): everything else, including design records
- [Agentic Loops manual](https://github.com/debozkurt/loop-guide): the paired concepts course loopkit implements
